108.status: Recorded just one out against Dodgers on June 6 and said on June 7 his elbow had been bothering him his last three starts when throwing changeups. Scheduled to undergo an MRI exam on June 8.
Scratched from lineup against the Dodgers on June 5 after sustaining injury swinging during batting practice. Held out of lineup on June 6 and again on June 7; hopeful he'll avoid IL.
Sustained injury playing with Double-A Rocket City on May 31. Added to 40-man roster because he had an opt-out in his Minor League contract and placed on IL.
Participated in light on-field activity on June 1 and June 2 before progressing to running and hitting off a tee from the left side on June 3.
Left April 29 outing before the start of the third inning. Scheduled to start his throwing program in early June with a return date shortly after the All-Star break.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Said he felt a pop and but has started to put pressure on his foot while wearing a boot.

Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Eligible to return from the 60-day IL May 26. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and resumed his throwing program.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Exited June 5 game at Toronto in the sixth inning as a precaution. MRI came back clean. Team is hopeful he will avoid the injured list.
Traveled to Baltimore to have back examined on June 5, then joined team in Toronto. Threw on June 6, but still getting other opinions. Not yet known whether he'll make his next start on June 8.
Completed first bullpen session on May 30 at Camden Yards, throwing 15-20 pitches. Threw another bullpen on June 5 in Toronto. Could start rehab assignment week of June 8.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29 Progressed to live batting practice during week of June 1.
Scratched from lineup ahead of May 12 game vs. the Yankees after feeling discomfort. Underwent an MRI and was placed on IL the next day. Hitting in cage as of June 5.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to left forearm discomfort. Was examined by Dr. Keith Meister and received cortisone shot on May 19.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continuing to discuss treatment options. No clear timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Expected to recover in time to return during the early part of the 2027 season.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.

111.status: Likely to be activated for the middle game of a three-game set at Tropicana Field on June 9, following a successful Minor League rehab outing for for Triple-A Worcester on June 6.
Will make second start of his latest Minor League rehab assignment on June 11, continuing his build-up to a starter's workload.
Move to 60-day IL was just procedural. The 60 days will expire in late June, before he will be ready to return. Idled from throwing until lat strain is asymptomatic.
Is in the early stages of a hitting program, but not close to a return.
Given the projected 8-to-12 week timetable to return following his May 21 surgery, Story won't be eligible to be activated until just after the All-Star break.
Believed to be a nagging injury that shouldn't keep him out for long.
Not progressing as hoped and will soon get checked out by team's medical staff.
Will soon start forearm strengthening exercises with an eye toward throwing by mid-June.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.

112.status: Exited his start June 7 after issuing a leadoff walk in the second inning, departing with training staff.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Iowa on June 2. Cubs want Shaw to play multiple games in a row (number of games TBD) prior to being activated.
Left knee meniscectomy (May 7). Logged 63 pitches in four innings (4 H, 3 R, 2 ER, 1 BB, 2 K) in a Minor League rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on May 31. Next rehab start is expected June 6.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 3 prospect, No. 86 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Resumed throwing program in late April and has since advanced to throwing bullpen sessions in Arizona.
Cleared to begin strengthening exercises on May 26.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per manager Craig Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

114.status: Started rehab assignment at Triple-A Columbus, playing five innings at shortstop. The plan is to get built up while playing multiple positions. “We just want to have Gabby prepared for whatever the situation/role is,” manager Stephen Vogt said.
MRI results confirmed inflammation but revealed no structural damage. Feeling better and working out, but no precise timeline yet for return.

115.status: A day after Freeman took a 98.2 mph pitch off the helmet from Milwaukee's Jacob Misiorowski and immediately exited the game, Rockies president of baseball operations Paul DePodesta said, "He seems to be doing well. We don't think it's terribly serious, but we're going to spend some time with him today."

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continuing a throwing program at the team's complex in Scottsdale, Ariz., but has not been cleared to face hitters.
Threw a simulated game on June 6. Currently scheduled for two rehab appearances, one for one inning and the second for more.
Simulated game on June 6 went well, with no tightness or discomfort. Scheduled for two one-inning rehab games for Triple-A Albuquerque next week.
Had been scheduled to start on June 6 against the Brewers after struggling in a May 31 start against the Giants.
Manager Warren Schaeffer said on June 5 that Herrera is receiving a second opinion on the UCL tear.
Manager Warren Schaeffer said Beck is still recovering from the injection he received in late May.

Did some hitting off the tee when the team was in Anaheim and has progressed to throwing and jogging. Change-of-direction drills, which have not been scheduled, will be the test to see if he is ready for an injury rehab assignment.
Feeling better but has not progressed to baseball activities.
Has received a PRP shot in his elbow to promote healing, according to manager Warren Schaeffer.
On a regimen of rest and treatment. He and the club decided against attempting to accelerate healing with a PRP injection.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 15 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Threw five scoreless innings with six strikeouts in a rehab start for High-A West Michigan on June 7. The Tigers hope to activate him for the series in Cleveland this weekend.
Will make a rehab start for Single-A Lakeland on June 9 at Clearwater.
Will make a second rehab start at Triple-A Toledo on June 10 vs. St. Paul. Expected to stretch out to 75 pitches or five or six innings.
Scheduled to make a rehab appearance at Triple-A Toledo on June 9.
Threw a bullpen session in front of Tigers coaches on June 2 at Tropicana Field. Not close to facing hitters yet.
Started a return-to-play throwing progression on June 2 following a cortisone injection in late May.
Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29. Saw a specialist and is shut down from throwing.
Began a throwing progression on May 14 after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

117.status: Was removed from the June 7 win over the A's while legging out a single in the third inning.
Will make fourth and final Minor League rehab start June 10 at Triple-A Sugar Land and should be stretched out to about 75 pitches.

Will make second rehab start June 9 at Triple-A Sugar Land. He allowed two runs, two hits, two walks and struck out two in two-thirds of an inning in his first rehab start June 3 with Double-A Corpus Christi.
Working out in in West Palm Beach, Fla., beginning therapeutic exercises.
Is hitting, throwing and catching bullpens and is close to a rehab assignment.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. Will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.
Has begun a throwing progression. Was scratched before his scheduled start against the Twins in Minneapolis on May 19.
Has started a throwing progression. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.

117.status: Has progressed to throwing live batting practice in West Palm Beach, Fla. after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing live batting practice in West Palm Beach, Fla. after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Hit by wild pitch on June 5 that cracked thumb nail and led to swelling, making it hard to grip bat and throw. Out of lineup both June 6 and 7, but team is hopeful he'll improve in coming days.

Crashed into the right-field wall on June 5, but remained in the game and was in the lineup the next day. Exited in the fourth inning on June 6 after favoring his shoulder during swings. Not in lineup June 7.
Resumed playing catch June 1 and is progressing well, but has not thrown a bullpen yet.
Suffered shoulder injury while rehabbing foot injury May 6. Began playing catch again on May 27 and is on track with his throwing progression, although it's still in the early stages.
On track with his bullpen schedule, including a four "up" session the week of June 1.
Imaging done May 29 revealed inflammation in shoulder but nothing more severe. Team is hopeful he'll feel better by early June to resume throwing.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: After missing back-to-back games with neck stiffness, Smith is considered day to day to open the Pittsburgh series. Imaging on his neck showed nothing of concern.
Still limited by back spasms and not playing catch as his recovery takes longer than hoped.
Set to throw another live batting practice session before embarking on a rehab assignment. Recovery hinges on post-session foot response during defensive drills.
Began a throwing program on June 5, two and a half weeks after undergoing surgery.
Joined Triple-A Oklahoma to begin a rehab assignment on June 4. Will likely take the maximum 30 days to build up.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but he has had trouble ramping up.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back surgery
119.status: Underwent a surgical procedure on his back, he shared on social media on May 31.
MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain. Expecting to miss roughly a month.
Began a throwing progression during the week of May 25.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: Throwing plyo balls against the wall, and next step will be playing catch.
Threw two scoreless innings with four strikeouts on June 5 in a Florida Complex League game.
Threw a simulated game on June 2 at Nationals Park.
Continuing throwing program at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., and threw a bullpen session of 30 pitches on May 26.
Continuing throwing progression at Nationals' training complex.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.

121.status: Was shut down from rehab with Triple-A Syracuse on June 6 due to left ankle soreness while going through running progression, per manager Carlos Mendoza. Will return to New York to be reevaluated.
Caught seven innings in back-to-back games for Triple-A Syracuse June 4 and 5. Could rejoin team during next homestand.
Has allowed seven earned runs over 12 innings across three rehab starts. Expected to start with Double-A Binghamton June 9. Will need to show better results before club considers activating him.
Started running on June 4. Timetable will become more specific once he restarts baseball activities.
Started baseball activities -- fielding drills, running sprints and swinging the bat in the indoor cages -- in late May but is not ready for a rehab assignment.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.
Timeline will remain fluid until symptoms subside. Was progressing better as of a May 29 update and could advance to baseball activities in the relatively near future.

Fractured his leg on a comebacker May 15. Will need 6-8 weeks to heal and another six or so for a Spring Training-style build-up. That should take him well past the All-Star break in a best-case scenario.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Went 3-for-5 with a home run and two RBIs on June 6 in his sixth Minor League rehab game with Triple-A Las Vegas.
Went 0-for-2 with a walk and an RBI while playing designated hitter in his first Minor League rehab game with Triple-A Las Vegas on June 6.
MRI on June 1 revealed a strain of the shoulder capsule and subscapularis muscle. Will receive PRP injection on June 4 and be re-evaluated 4-6 weeks from that date.
Began a throwing progression program on June 1. No timetable for a return.
Shut down from throwing for a minimum of three weeks, beginning May 18. Received an ultrasound guided cortisone injection on May 19.

Pulled from rehab assignment after sustaining a "high-grade" hamstring strain on May 19. Was scheduled to receive a platelet-rich plasma injection May 26 and will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day on June 7 to make room for infielder Davis Wendzel on the 40-man roster.
Experienced elbow soreness after game on May 27 and was diagnosed with a minor right forearm strain. Expected to spend minimal time on IL.

Suffered apparent injury on May 9 in San Francisco. Foot began swelling during flight to Pittsburgh on May 10. Cleared by doctor on May 29 to resume weight lifting activities in shoes. Will be reassessed during June 1-4 trip to Houston.

135.status: Has dealt with a nagging hamstring for much of the season. Felt it again legging out an infield single on June 7. Was removed for a pinch-runner. Unclear if an IL stint will be necessary.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Has resumed light baseball activity and is ramping up. Was able to watch game from dugout for the first time on June 6.
Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5. Making notable progress -- is swinging pain-free and catching bullpens. Could begin a rehab stint in the coming days.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn labrum in his right hip on June 5. First sustained the injury in 2021, but it recurred and worsened over the season's first two months. Likely to miss the remainder of the season.
Began experiencing knee soreness in late May, and landed on the IL when it worsened. Team is optimistic his absence won't be lengthy.
Began a Minor League rehab stint on June 2, pitching 2 1/3 scoreless innings for Triple-A El Paso.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Began playing catch in late May.
Expected to pitch again this season, after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Began playing catch in late May. No timeline for a return to the mound.
Placed on the injured list one day after working two scoreless innings in relief at Milwaukee. Began a throwing program on May 23. Could return as a reliever.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Hit by 95.6 mph fastball on June 5. Was out of lineup June 6-7, continuing treatment and playing catch. Hitting is final hurdle, due to bat’s pressure on his hand.
Went 0-for-3 at DH in the first game of a rehab assignment on June 5 with High-A Everett.
Hitting 5-for-17 in rehab assignment Triple-A Tacoma through June 3 (5 games). Slated to play nine innings on June 5.
Currently running on a weightless treadmill, and will be assessed for a running program in the next few days. Likely to return to Seattle for a check-in during the team's next road trip.More »
Undergoing an MRI on May 29 to make sure everything is clear before ramping up his throwing program.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Underwent surgery in Arizona on June 4 to remove the fractured hamate bone from his left wrist.
Landed on the IL after experiencing tenderness between his right quad and knee.
Hasn't played for Triple-A Sacramento since May 23 due to the wrist fracture; will be re-examined in two weeks.
Will miss two to three weeks with the adductor strain.
Received a PRP injection on May 19 in San Francisco. Continuing to rehab in Arizona and began a hitting progression June 1.
Sustained the injury fielding a ground ball between the mound and first base on May 31.
Currently in a walking boot and will see Dr. Robert Anderson for a second opinion later this week.
Began a throwing progression on June 1 and will return to the mound later this week.
Has tossed 6 2/3 scoreless innings in his first six rehab appearances for Triple-A Sacramento.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 30.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 19.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Had a planned day off June 6 during his rehab assignment with Memphis and is expected to play nine innings in center field on June 7.
Had a setback during his rehab and is now dealing with a left elbow issue similar to the right elbow injury that sent him to the IL.
Fernandez woke up with back tightness and received an injection.

139.status: Felt stiffness in his wrist while playing catch on May 29. Rather than pitching through discomfort, team decided to give him a few days off from throwing to heal. Resumed playing catch June 3 and threw a bullpen session on June 6.

Signed to a Major League contract on April 14. Began a rehab assignment with one-inning outings for the Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays on May 23 and May 26. Threw two scoreless innings for Triple-A Durham on June 2.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he sustained while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return.

He reported soreness in back of right shoulder on April 15. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Had a setback while playing catch from 75 feet, so he underwent a PRP injection. "Going to be a while," manager Kevin Cash said.

Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, with goal to return in June or July. Throwing bullpens and making progress, but no decision on when he will progress to facing hitters.
Felt soreness during recent series in New York and while pitching in Baltimore on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation, but should resume throwing around June 1.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Playing catch with the goal to start bullpen sessions on June 8.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11 then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Not taking part in baseball activities, so no timeline for return.
Scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on the IL one day later. Cash said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after surgery. Not taking part in workouts as of May 29.

Felt his hamstring “grab” while running to first base on an infield single May 22. Did not feel better running first to third and exited the game. MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain that will sideline him for 6-8 weeks.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

140.status: Felt something in his arm after a June 1 start in St. Louis. Scans showed elbow inflammation and he will continue to be monitored over the next 10 days.
Threw a 20-pitch live batting practice session on June 3.

140.injury: Right glute strain, wrist inflammation, viral meningitis
140.status: Took his first live batting practice on June 3. A rehab stint could begin the week of June 8.

Martin missed 30 games (April 15 to May 19) previously. He allowed four earned runs in four appearances (four innings) since returning, surrendering seven hits and two walks without recording a strikeout.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23. Moved to 60-day IL on May 31.
Came out of the game on May 23 feeling fatigue and soreness in his shoulder, and woke up next day feeling it even more.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.

141.status: Still dealing with inflammation in left wrist after leaving June 5 game. This is a "familiar" issue to Varsho and the Blue Jays do not currently expect an IL stint.
Threw one inning in a Triple-A rehab game June 5 and could be nearing his MLB return.

141.injury: Right forearm tendinitis and left ankle inflammation
141.status: Threw 73 pitches over 3 2/3 innings for Triple-A Buffalo on June 5.
Threw 75 pitches (50 strikes) during rehab start with Triple-A Buffalo on June 4 and could "very likely" return to the Blue Jays' rotation for his next start.
Catching Friday in Dunedin and will continue to build up on rehab assignment.
Will pitch Sunday in Dunedin and could soon be nearing the end of his long rehab process.
Continues to hit and throw down at the complex in Florida.
Will make rehab start with Triple-A Buffalo on June 6.
Began hitting progression in Dunedin, Fla.
Will be having a scope surgical procedure on his knee after receiving a second medical opinion. It is not believed to be season-ending.
Transferred to 60-day IL as recovery continues for right shoulder impingement.
Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Held out of lineup again on June 7 but is trending well and could play following the June 8 off-day.
Threw a bullpen on June 7. Next step will likely be either a second live batting practice or a rehab start.
Began rehabilitation assignment in Florida Complex League on June 6. Started in right field, went 0-for-1 with three walks and a steal.
Scratched from scheduled rehab appearance June 5. Played catch June 6 and next steps to be determined, but could still progress to another rehab appearance in next few days.
Has begun a throwing program, and thanks to short layoff, could progress quickly from there to game action.
Underwent surgery on May 20. Cast has been removed and he has begun throwing, but he is not yet swinging a bat.
Diagnosed with mild right flexor strain. Will be shut down for 10-14 days and re-evaluated then.
Underwent surgery to repair UCL in thumb and will likely miss another month.
Has begun throwing progression, still three weeks from throwing off a mound.

142.status: Has begin hitting off a machine and doing defensive work. Hopes to take live BP this week and could begin rehab assignment next week.

143.injury: Discogenic pain and facet inflammation in back
143.status: Underwent procedure on June 5 to address recurring back issue. Phillies hope he returns to Minor League game action in six to eight weeks.
Playing catch out to 105 feet. Has started the throwing program portion of building back up, per Don Mattingly.

144.status: Exited a June 5 game with minor discomfort and was left out of the lineup on June 6. Manager Walt Weiss said the ailment isn’t anything serious.
Could be available in the second half of July. Was called for catcher's interference when Hyeseong Kim's bat hit his glove during May 10 game at Dodger Stadium.

Threw two scoreless innings (2 BBs) for the Florida Coast League team June 1. First time pitching in a game since undergoing arthroscopic surgery Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from right elbow.
Manager Walt Weiss said the catcher could return during the next homestand, which runs June 16-21.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. Began throwing in early May and is up to 75 feet in his throwing progression.
Has started throwing bullpen sessions, but hasn't been cleared to begin facing hitters. He underwent surgery on June 9, 2025.
An MRI revealed a strain in Farmer’s right forearm, placing him on the 10-day injured list on May 18.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Teel has started hitting off a tee and done some catching drills, but none of the high-intensity drills needed to get back on the field, according to manager Will Venable.
Murakami received a platelet-rich plasma injection on June 1. He suffered a Grade 2 strain running out a fielder’s-choice grounder in the third inning of a 4-3 victory over the Tigers on May 29.
Hays was pulled from his injury rehab assignment and sent back to Chicago for further evaluation on June 1 after continuing to experience lower-body soreness.
Has felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.
Manager Will Venable expects Schultz’s IL stint to be minimal.
Threw on May 25, but still needs to build up arm strength.
Recently underwent an appendectomy, but has resumed throwing. The procedure did delay his rehab from Tommy John surgery, though.
Had a setback in his rehab process likely pushing him into the second half of the season. His timeline is uncertain at this point.
Hicks had felt general soreness in the area for close to two weeks. But it grew increasingly worse, so the White Sox made the move.
First injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Estimated timeline for return was 12-16 weeks from date of injury.
Will begin throwing soon, according to manager Clayton McCullough. Able to stand up and walk on his own. Sustained the injury during stretching in between innings on May 27.

Must get through multiple days in a row of high-intensity running before beginning rehab assignment, per manager Clayton McCullough. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.
Injured during warm-up drills on May 31, returned to Miami for treatment June 1. Timeline for return to play is approximately six weeks.
First felt the discomfort in mid-April in Atlanta but it never affected his pitching, just the build-up. Had trouble breathing leading up to testing. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1.More >
Two-to-three-week timetable. Had been dealing with soreness since May 9 and it hadn't gotten better.More >

146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Underwent testing June 6 after complaining of neck issue and headaches June 5. Concussion test was negative. Scheduled to undergo additional tests, including MRI, on June 8. Will not travel with team.

Threw June 1 and 2 from 75 feet and "seems to be responding to everything," manager Aaron Boone said. Aiming to increase throwing program to 120 feet week of June 7. Set to be re-imaged week of June 7 in hopes of being cleared for mound work.

147.injury: Stress fracture of first rib on right side
147.status: Believes injury was initially sustained April 26. After a period of rest and recovery, will be evaluated again in 4-6 weeks to determine level of healing and appropriate next steps.
Began rehab assignment June 5 with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Is expected to play outfield June 7, then continue in Minors for several more games, GM Brian Cashman said.
Took live batting practice on June 3 and is scheduled to do so again June 6. Has been performing agility exercises and running lightly. Could return in 2-3 weeks, GM Brian Cashman said June 5.
Tossed live batting practice to Jasson Domínguez and Giancarlo Stanton on June 3 at Yankee Stadium. Scheduled to toss to Stanton again on June 6.
Most recent mound session was 20 pitches as of June 2 and said he is “feeling great.” Could face hitters by the end of June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.

158.status: Exited June 5 appearance warming up for the seventh inning. Will get second opinion before determining if Tommy John surgery is needed. (last updated: June 6)
Exited Brewers' June 4 game against the Giants. Manager Pat Murphy said Hall has a “return to play” timeline of four to six weeks. (last updated: June 5)

Returned from the IL on May 31 only to feel discomfort during a pregame throwing session and land back on the IL. MRI scan showed trap strain. Will see a specialist to further pinpoint a diagnosis. (last updated: June 3)
Threw 42 pitches while simulating three innings in a live batting practice session on June 3. The Brewers planned to discuss next steps with Woodruff following the outing. (last updated: June 3)
Took live at-bats against rehabbing Brewers starter Brandon Woodruff on June 3 and is nearing a rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville, manager Pat Murphy said. (last updated: June 3)

Threw 3 2/3 innings "without issue" in his first start in the rookie-level Arizona Complex League on June 2. The Brewers moved him to the ACL to take advantage of the league's re-entry rule if needed. (last updated: June 3)
Received a facet injection in his back on June 2 to relieve inflammation. (last updated: June 3)
Threw eight of 10 pitches for strikes for Triple-A Nashville on May 29 in the first of what was expected to be a multi-outing rehab assignment. (last updated: May 30)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
